# io.singer_window { #tspaint.io.singer_window }

```python
tspaint.io.singer_window(
    source,
    *,
    start,
    end,
    out_prefix,
    _Ne,
    _m=None,
    _r=None,
    _ratio=None,
    _n_samples=20,
    _thin=10,
    _ploidy=1,
    _seed=42,
    _polar=0.5,
    _penalty=None,
    _hmm_epsilon=None,
    _psmc_bins=None,
    _recomb_map=None,
    _mut_map=None,
    _fast=False,
    singer_args=None,
    max_retries=50,
    singer_bin=None,
)
```

Run SINGER on ONE genomic window ``[start, end)``, leaving the raw node/branch/mut tables.

The per-window unit of a GWF window × member parallelisation: run this for each window, then
stitch the per-window tables for each MCMC member into a chromosome-length ARG with
:func:`build_merge_table` + :func:`run_merge_arg`. Uses the **bare SINGER binary** (not
``singer_master``, whose ``-m`` rate path is broken in the bundled copy — an ``arggs.ploidy``
typo and a dead ``-mut_map`` branch).

## Parameters {.doc-section .doc-section-parameters}

<code>[**source**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[tskit](`tskit`).[TreeSequence](`tskit.TreeSequence`) or [str](`str`)]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   Genotypes: a tree sequence (a haploid VCF is written next to ``out_prefix``), or a VCF path (used directly — SINGER reads ``<prefix>.vcf``; no rewrite, so a GWF grid shares it).

<code>[**start**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[float](`float`)]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   Window bounds passed to SINGER ``-start`` / ``-end``.

<code>[**end**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[float](`float`)]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   Window bounds passed to SINGER ``-start`` / ``-end``.

<code>[**out_prefix**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[str](`str`)]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   Output prefix; SINGER writes ``{out_prefix}_nodes_<i>.txt`` etc.

<code>[**_Ne**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} []{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   The underscore-prefixed SINGER flags (plus the retry / binary controls), as for :func:`singer`.

<code>[**_m**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} []{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   The underscore-prefixed SINGER flags (plus the retry / binary controls), as for :func:`singer`.

<code>[**_r**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} []{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   The underscore-prefixed SINGER flags (plus the retry / binary controls), as for :func:`singer`.

<code>[**_n_samples**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} []{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   The underscore-prefixed SINGER flags (plus the retry / binary controls), as for :func:`singer`.

<code>[**_thin**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} []{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   The underscore-prefixed SINGER flags (plus the retry / binary controls), as for :func:`singer`.

<code>[**_ploidy**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} []{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   The underscore-prefixed SINGER flags (plus the retry / binary controls), as for :func:`singer`.

<code>[**_seed**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} []{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   The underscore-prefixed SINGER flags (plus the retry / binary controls), as for :func:`singer`.

<code>[**_polar**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} []{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   The underscore-prefixed SINGER flags (plus the retry / binary controls), as for :func:`singer`.

<code>[**max_retries**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} []{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   The underscore-prefixed SINGER flags (plus the retry / binary controls), as for :func:`singer`.

<code>[**singer_bin**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} []{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   The underscore-prefixed SINGER flags (plus the retry / binary controls), as for :func:`singer`.

## Returns {.doc-section .doc-section-returns}

<code>[]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[list](`list`)\[[int](`int`)\]]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   The MCMC sample indices written for this window.
